algorithms.title: Shock Recognition and Category-Matched Management
algorithms.description: Recognizing shock before hypotension develops and matching treatment to the underlying category.
steps: Look for compensated shock signs even with a normal blood pressure: pale skin, tachycardia, tachypnea.
Do not wait for hypotension to begin treatment — falling BP signals decompensated (late-stage) shock.
Identify the shock category from history and exam: hypovolemic (bleeding/fluid loss), distributive (septic/anaphylactic/neurogenic), cardiogenic (pump failure), or obstructive (mechanical).

Match treatment to category: fluids for hypovolemic; epinephrine for anaphylactic; source control/fluids/antibiotics per protocol for septic; inotropes/vasopressors with cautious fluids for cardiogenic; mechanical correction for obstructive.
Keep the patient warm (blanket) throughout — hypothermia worsens coagulopathy and cardiac function.
Reassess frequently for progression from compensated to decompensated shock.
